snapshot.note: Score against this fixed snapshot. Later view drift is acceptable, but the canonical challenge-video set below is fixed for grading.

channel.name: MrBeast
channel.collection_method: Channel /videos tab snapshot with manual challenge-prize filtering applied to high-view videos.

challenge_rules.canonical: 456 contestants play recreated Squid Game-style elimination rounds until one final player wins the cash prize.
allowed_synonyms: 456 players compete through Squid Game games for a single winner
last surviving contestant wins the money after multiple elimination games

canonical_videos.title: Ages 1 - 100 Fight For $500,000

challenge_rules.canonical: 100 contestants, one for each age from 1 to 100, compete through elimination rounds until one finalist wins $500,000.
allowed_synonyms: 100 contestants of different ages get eliminated round by round
one contestant from every age fights for the final prize

key_stages.canonical: Contestants begin in age-numbered cubes / cells
A series of elimination rounds reduces the field dramatically
The video ends with a briefcase / final-decision finish

uncertainty: Do not require exact recall of every intermediate mini-game. It is enough if the answer captures the age-based elimination structure and the final briefcase-style finish.

canonical_videos.title: 50 YouTubers Fight For $1,000,000

challenge_rules.canonical: 50 YouTubers compete through multiple elimination games until one creator wins the grand prize for their subscribers / community.
allowed_synonyms: 50 creators battle through mini-games for the final million
50 YouTubers are eliminated round by round until one remains
creator competition with elimination rounds and a million-dollar headline prize

key_stages.canonical: Multiple elimination rounds cut the field down to a final group
allowed_synonyms: field narrows to a final few

key_stages.canonical: The endgame includes negotiation / deal-making tension around the prize

prize.canonical: Headline prize: $1,000,000. Also accept answers that correctly explain a later retained amount of $700,000 after a side deal.

uncertainty: Accept either the headline prize '$1,000,000' or a clearly justified '$700,000 after a side deal' answer. The response should show it understands why those numbers differ.

canonical_videos.title: Survive 100 Days Trapped, Win $500,000

challenge_rules.canonical: Two strangers are trapped together for 100 days and must make it to the end; the finale resolves with a split-or-steal style prize decision.
allowed_synonyms: two people must survive 100 days trapped together
Bailey and Suzie endure isolation for 100 days
endurance challenge for two contestants with a final split decision

key_stages.canonical: Long isolation / endurance period inside the trapped environment
Temptations and spending choices reduce the possible final payout
allowed_synonyms: offers that shrink the prize pot

key_stages.canonical: The ending is a split-or-steal / split-decision finale

winner.canonical: Bailey and Suzie as co-winners after choosing to split

prize.canonical: Headline challenge prize is $500,000, but the accepted resolved payout is a split of the reduced final pot: $179,949.50 each / $359,899 total.
accepted_values: $500,000 challenge prize split at the end

uncertainty: Many summaries remember only the headline '$500,000'. Give full credit only if the answer clearly states that the final outcome is a split. Accept the exact reduced payout or a correct high-level 'they split the remaining prize' summary.

canonical_videos.title: Survive 100 Days In Circle, Win $500,000

challenge_rules.canonical: One main challenger must stay inside a large circle for 100 days; leaving the circle loses the challenge and surviving all 100 days wins the prize.
allowed_synonyms: stay inside the circle for 100 days to win
single endurance challenge inside a giant circle
if he leaves the circle he loses, if he lasts 100 days he wins

key_stages.canonical: The challenge is pure endurance inside the circle over 100 days
Temptations, weather, or comfort trade-offs try to make the challenger leave
The challenger completes the full 100 days and wins

uncertainty: Treat guest visitors and helpers as non-contestants. The graded participant count is one main challenger.

explicit_exclusions.title: Last To Leave Circle Wins $500,000
explicit_exclusions.exclude_reason: Do not accept for this task variant. Even though it is a challenge video, this benchmark fixes a different self-contained set.

explicit_exclusions.title: $1 vs $500,000 Plane Ticket!
explicit_exclusions.exclude_reason: Prize-showcase / comparison video, not a challenge-prize competition with a resolved winner.

explicit_exclusions.title: $1 vs $100,000,000 Car!
explicit_exclusions.exclude_reason: Prize-showcase / comparison video, not a challenge-prize competition with a resolved winner.

explicit_exclusions.title: $1 vs $1,000,000,000 Yacht!
explicit_exclusions.exclude_reason: Prize-showcase / comparison video, not a challenge-prize competition with a resolved winner.
